name: Pages
summary: The Pages class is a container class for all pages in a Document.

methods.summary: The #[] method returns a value from the array of Layout::Pages.
methods.remarks: The #[] method returns a value from the array of Layout::Pages.

params.name: index
params.doc: The index of the Layout::Page to return.

throws.when: if index is out of range

methods.name: add
methods.summary: The #add method adds a new Layout::Page to the Document.
methods.remarks: The #add method adds a new Layout::Page to the Document. The newly added Layout::Page will be the last one in the Document.

params.name: name
params.doc: The name for the new page.

methods.name: each
methods.summary: Note: Don't remove content from this collection while iterating over it with #each.
methods.remarks: Note: Don't remove content from this collection while iterating over it with #each. This would change the size of the collection and cause elements to be skipped as the indices change. Instead copy the current collection to an array using to_a and then use each on the array, when removing content. The #each method iterates through all of the Layout::Pages.

methods.name: index
methods.summary: The #index method returns the index of the Layout::Page, or nil if it doesn't exist in the Document.
methods.remarks: The #index method returns the index of the Layout::Page, or nil if it doesn't exist in the Document.

methods.name: length
methods.signature: length => Integer Also known as: size
methods.summary: The #length method returns the number of Layout::Pages.
methods.remarks: The #length method returns the number of Layout::Pages.

methods.name: remove
methods.summary: The #remove method deletes the given Layout::Page from the Document.
methods.remarks: The #remove method deletes the given Layout::Page from the Document.

throws.when: if the Layout::Page is not in the Document
if the Layout::Page is the only one in the Document

methods.name: reorder
methods.signature: reorder(page, new_index) => Object | reorder(index, new_index) => Object
methods.summary: The #reorder method moves a Layout::Page to a different index within the Document's list of pages.
methods.remarks: The #reorder method moves a Layout::Page to a different index within the Document's list of pages. This will move the Layout::Page such that its new index becomes new_index.

params.name: new_index
params.doc: The index to put the Layout::Page at

throws.when: if the Layout::Page is not in the Document
if new_index is out of range

properties.name: initial
properties.summary: The #initial method returns the initial Layout::Page that will be displayed the next time the Document is opened.
properties.remarks: The #initial method returns the initial Layout::Page that will be displayed the next time the Document is opened. This value will change whenever the Layout::Page is changed in the Document in LayOut.
